creator: shige@iee.niit.ac.jp
text:
I used freebsd-update to upgrade OS version 13.0-p11 to 13.1-RELEASE, but it fails.

% uname -a
FreeBSD hp7700 13.0-RELEASE-p11 FreeBSD 13.0-RELEASE-p11 #0: Tue Apr  5 18:52:04 UTC 2022     root@amd64-builder.daemonology.net:/usr/obj/usr/src/i386.i386/sys/GENERIC  i386

#freebsd-update -r 13.1-RELEASE upgrade
Looking up update.FreeBSD.org mirrors... 2 mirrors found.
Fetching public key from update2.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching metadata signature for 13.0-RELEASE from update2.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching metadata index... done.
Fetching 2 metadata files... done.
Inspecting system... done.
 ...
Fetching files from 13.0-RELEASE for merging... done.
Preparing to download files... done.
Fetching 17832 patches.....10....20....30....40....50....60....70....80....90...
 ...
....17830. done.
Applying patches... done.
Fetching 3135 files...
 ...
2920....2930....2940....2950.. gunzip: (stdin): unexpected end of file
e0904ee257f4bee67b75f7156b864d536ba0ed3f28a3eadb4650aaa6b18f5e27 has incorrect hash.

I tried sometimes with "rm -rf /var/db/freebsd-update/*", but they fail with the same message in every times.


#ls -l /var/db/freebsd-update/e0904ee257f4bee67b75f7156b864d536ba0ed3f28a3eadb4650aaa6b18f5e27.gz
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  1190 Aug 18 13:17 /var/db/freebsd-update/e0904ee257f4bee67b75f7156b864d536ba0ed3f28a3eadb4650aaa6b18f5e27.gz


#file /var/db/freebsd-update/e0904ee257f4bee67b75f7156b864d536ba0ed3f28a3eadb4650aaa6b18f5e27.gz
/var/db/freebsd-update/e0904ee257f4bee67b75f7156b864d536ba0ed3f28a3eadb4650aaa6b18f5e27.gz: gzip compressed data, last modified: Thu May 12 08:35:11 2022, from Unix, original size modulo 2^32 634299799
